hero.question: Will the DHS shutdown end after March 31, 2026?
hero.prob_take: Two-thirds of the market thinks the DHS shutdown ends by March 31, 2026, which assumes Congress actually negotiates instead of kicking the can down the road again, and that seems optimistic given we're in an election year. The real tell is the 10-point jump in 24 hours, likely driven by some headline noise, but shutdowns this late in an administration historically either resolve fast or drag into the next term, making NO the better asymmetric bet at these odds.

daily_take.headline: DHS Shutdown Odds Spike 10 Points. Is the Market Pricing in Capitulation?
daily_take.deck: The 66% odds that a DHS shutdown ends after March 31 just jumped 10 points in 24 hours on $955K volume, signaling serious money is betting on a deal. But here's the trap: Congress has a track record of last-minute theater followed by actual capitulation. Smart bettors should ask if 66% reflects real resolution odds or just "we'll fund it eventually" thinking. The sharper play might be shorting this bounce and waiting for real leverage to emerge in late March.
